title: Reproducible Builds, CI Gates, Artifacts, GitHub Pages, and Promotion · Language Clinic: Postdeploy Smoke
summary: CI downloads floating tools, embeds current time, deploys from a dirty workspace, rebuilds after approval, grants broad token permissions, and publishes preview origins as canonical URLs. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the postdeploy smoke increment in immutable reproducible staged deployment pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.
Package the public root correctly, upload a Pages artifact, deploy only from an authorized event, and verify base path, custom domain, HTTPS, and not-found behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-ci-deployment-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-ci-deployment-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_ci_deployment_3(clean_builds: int = 2, equal_digests: bool = True, artifact_promotions: int = 1, rebuilds_after_approval: int = 0, smoke_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if clean_builds < 2:
        return False, clean_builds, "reproducibility-sample"
    if not equal_digests:
        return False, clean_builds, "digest-drift"
    if artifact_promotions != 1 or rebuilds_after_approval > 0:
        return False, artifact_promotions, "promotion-boundary"
    if smoke_failures > 0:
        return False, smoke_failures, "postdeploy-failure"
    assert artifact_promotions == 1
    return True, clean_builds, "immutable-deployment"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Postdeploy Smoke evidence · Theory Postdeploy Smoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-postdeploy-smoke-vc6m2l" that uses case 9009 to promote one reproducible least-authority artifact through deployment and post-release smoke checks. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Postdeploy Smoke evidence · Theory Postdeploy Smoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Postdeploy Smoke into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Postdeploy Smoke evidence · Theory Postdeploy Smoke, keep Postdeploy Smoke connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-postdeploy-smoke-vc6m2l
# Competency: ssg-postdeploy-smoke.
# Case 9009: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_postdeploy_smoke_vc6m2l(clean_builds: int = 2, equal_digests: bool = True, artifact_promotions: int = 1, rebuilds_after_approval: int = 0, smoke_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vc6m2l = "vc6m2l-l2m6cv-9009"
    assert evidence_variant_vc6m2l
    evidence_axes_vc6m2l = (
        "readervc6m2l",
        "source9009",
        "parsel2m6cv",
        "routevc6m2l9009",
        "artifact9009vc6m2l",
        "recoveryl2m6cv9009",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vc6m2l)) == 6
    if clean_builds < 2:
        return False, clean_builds, "reproducibility-sample"
    if not equal_digests:
        return False, clean_builds, "digest-drift"
    if artifact_promotions != 1 or rebuilds_after_approval > 0:
        return False, artifact_promotions, "promotion-boundary"
    if smoke_failures > 0:
        return False, smoke_failures, "postdeploy-failure"
    assert artifact_promotions == 1
    return True, clean_builds, "immutable-deployment"

At Static-site publishing and Python Build Postdeploy Smoke evidence · Theory Postdeploy Smoke, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3275 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to promote one reproducible least-authority artifact through deployment and post-release smoke checks; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Postdeploy Smoke model · Theory Postdeploy Smoke
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Postdeploy Smoke: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4894 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to promote one reproducible least-authority artifact through deployment and post-release smoke checks; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.
